other_observers: Peter Gent, Maggie Boswell, David Waltman, John Vanderpoel, Tom Delaney, Scott Severs; Found by Scott Roederer
description: A mid sized larid with a distinctive black M pattern in the wing tops and back, dark nuchal collar, and black terminal tail band, all visible despite the heavy snow and fairly long distance to the bird. The underwings appeared to be white, with wingtip details difficult to make out.Behaviors: The bird was only observed foraging up and down the rocky south shore with other gulls. It would wheel and dive down to join other gulls harassing mergansers, but couldnt be seen on the water. Its flight was distinctively different than the other gulls present, using lots of dynamic soaring compared to the more frenzied flapping of the other gulls.Call: not heardPlumage: juvenile
